stories.body: Ukraine’s newly appointed commander-in-chief Mykhailo Drapatyi is taking charge amid a manpower crunch, fraying command structures and a rapidly evolving drone battle, with President Volodymyr Zelenskiy asking him to ramp up offensive operations and technological upgrades even as Russian forces press in Donetsk and intensify missile strikes on Kyiv; analysts say the 43-year-old former ground forces chief is respected for integrity but must calm protests over his predecessor’s leadership while delineating roles between the General Staff and the defense ministry.
stories.headline: Ukraine's new top general tasked with fixing manpower gaps and drone warfare

stories.body: Fire crews in Spain and France warned of extremely high wildfire risk despite gains in containing Spain’s largest blaze of the year, which has charred about 32,000 hectares in Guadalajara and forced evacuations in more than 30 municipalities, while French authorities banned barbecues in heat-scorched Var after two firefighters died, meteorologists flagged 3°C-above-normal highs across Western Europe, and scientists linked the relentless heatwaves, drought and sudden storms to climate change.
stories.headline: Spain and France battle vast wildfires as heatwave keeps southern Europe on edge

stories.body: Ousted Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores returned to Manhattan federal court for a scheduling hearing in their U.S. narcoterrorism case, where both sides proposed a June 2027 trial date while defense lawyers signaled plans to seek dismissal on head-of-state immunity and legality of capture grounds, prosecutors prepared to turn over classified evidence by early 2027, and the Trump administration’s raid and subsequent easing of sanctions on Caracas continued to frame the geopolitical backdrop.
stories.headline: Maduro and wife return to U.S. court as prosecutors push for June 2027 trial

stories.body: U.S. officials warned that Yemen’s Houthi movement’s vow to stop ships from loading or unloading in Saudi ports could force Washington to divert naval and air assets already committed to confronting Iran, raising the prospect of a second front in the Red Sea that would strain munitions stockpiles and deployment schedules, even though the resilient Houthis have previously withstood Saudi-led and U.S. bombardments and would also struggle to resupply under the U.S. blockade of Iran.
stories.headline: Houthi threat to blockade Saudi ports would stretch U.S. forces already fighting Iran

stories.body: Former Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orbán issued a “Statement of Resistance” accusing the new center-right government of Peter Magyar of destroying democracy after prosecutors investigating alleged misappropriation at the National Culture Fund seized Fidesz servers, claiming the move was politically motivated and vowing peaceful resistance inside and outside parliament even as Magyar’s Tisza party wields a supermajority to roll back Orbán-era constitutional changes.
stories.headline: Orban urges supporters to 'restore democracy' after raids on Fidesz servers

stories.body: Bystanders recorded Madison, Wisconsin, police fatally shooting a man in the city’s Marquette neighborhood, and the footage circulated online, prompting immediate protests over the officer’s actions and adding to tensions as residents demand transparency about what led to the encounter and how the investigation will unfold.
stories.headline: Madison police shooting of man in Marquette neighborhood sparks protests

stories.body: Tropical Storm Bertha made landfall in Louisiana with heavy rain and storm surge that sent waves crashing onto New Orleans’ Lake Pontchartrain shoreline, pushed residents in coastal communities to prepare for flooding, and prompted West Virginia officials to warn of compounding flood risks from the storm’s inland remnants even as forecasters tracked the system’s westward move toward Texas.
stories.headline: Tropical Storm Bertha comes ashore in Louisiana, threatens flooding across Gulf Coast

stories.body: Former state representative Amish Shah won Arizona’s 1st District Democratic primary for the second consecutive cycle, overcoming party leadership’s backing of former broadcaster Marlene Galán-Woods in a battleground seat that President Trump carried by three points, setting up a general-election matchup with Trump-endorsed ex-NFL kicker Jay Feely after outgoing Republican David Schweikert opted to run for governor.
stories.headline: Amish Shah defeats DCCC favorite in key Arizona swing-seat Democratic primary

stories.body: Vox forced its “national priority” clause into the Valencian regional government’s €33.3 billion 2026 budget, marking the first Spanish spending plan to specify that certain housing and social aid programs prioritize nationals, after months of warning the Partido Popular it would withhold support without explicit language, prompting criticism from opposition parties alarmed by the far-right party’s leverage.
stories.headline: Valencian budget becomes Spain's first to enshrine Vox's 'national priority' rule

stories.body: Villages in Guadalajara’s Sierra Norte are slowly reopening after Spain’s largest recorded regional wildfire burned over 32,000 hectares in six blistering days, leaving charred landscapes, exhausted firefighters and residents describing the oncoming flames as “the sound of hell,” with some locals having stayed behind alongside crews to shield their homes while others remain evacuated or confined.
stories.headline: Residents begin returning after Guadalajara wildfire scorches more than 32,000 hectares

stories.body: Spain’s so-called Zapatero case escalated after former ally Julio Martínez Martínez contradicted ex-prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero’s emphatic denials of involvement in the Plus Ultra airline rescue, while current company president Julio Martínez Sola backed messages obtained from the previous CEO’s phone, undermining the defense strategy of challenging the evidence’s legality even as the government continues to stand by Zapatero.
stories.headline: Zapatero investigation widens as witnesses contradict ex-PM over Plus Ultra bailout

stories.body: Spain’s coalition partners PSOE and Sumar agreed on a housing decree that would let tenants extend current lease terms and freeze rents for up to two years through June 30, 2028 as part of a broader residential crisis package, but the draft still needs backing from ideologically diverse parliamentary allies such as Junts, PNV and Podemos, making final approval uncertain.
stories.headline: PSOE and Sumar strike deal to freeze rents through June 2028 despite uncertain votes

stories.body: A ruptured gas pipe at the Plaça de Sants metro accessibility works in Barcelona triggered a nighttime explosion and towering flame that injured eight workers—two seriously—and forced firefighters to cordon off Carrer de Sants while gas crews struggle to halt the flow feeding a blaze visible from several blocks away, disrupting shuttle buses and closing one station access though the L5 platforms remain open.
stories.headline: Eight workers injured as gas explosion ignites metro construction site in Plaça de Sants

stories.body: Part of a shaft wall at the Vallbona tunnel works for the R2 rail line caved in around midday, swallowing a crane, blocking road access near Plaça del Primer de Maig and suspending train service between Montcada i Reixac and Sant Andreu while Adif investigates the cause, injects cement to stabilize the terrain and shares constant structural monitoring data with Barcelona’s fire brigade.
stories.headline: Ground collapse at Vallbona R2 works halts trains between Montcada and Sant Andreu

stories.body: Barcelona mayor Jaume Collboni proposed asking Spain’s government to modify the Urban Leases Law so authorities can moderate commercial rent hikes in hotspots and, with Catalonia, set up a preemptive purchase mechanism to acquire strategic or emblematic storefronts, arguing that surging rents are pushing out viable neighborhood shops in favor of deeper-pocketed chains and eroding the city’s commercial identity.
stories.headline: Collboni proposes regulating commercial rents and creating a fund to buy strategic storefronts
